tossups.question: A set of 103 postcard-sized drawings by this artist, rediscovered in 2019, was commissioned for a never-published encyclopedia called The Great Picture Book of Everything. This artist used eight diagonal gray bands to represent a stream in which two mallards search for food. Late in life, this artist engaged in “daily exorcisms” involving painting a lion every day, and painted other big cats in late works like Tiger in the Snow. This artist predicted that his art would finally achieve a divine state at age 110 in the colophon to a series of books in which he called himself an “old man mad about painting.” That series includes over 100 depictions of a site that this artist also depicted in prints like Fine Wind, Clear Morning and Thunderstorm Beneath the Summit. For 10 points, name this artist of Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji.
tossups.question_sanitized: A set of 103 postcard-sized drawings by this artist, rediscovered in 2019, was commissioned for a never-published encyclopedia called The Great Picture Book of Everything. This artist used eight diagonal gray bands to represent a stream in which two mallards search for food. Late in life, this artist engaged in "daily exorcisms" involving painting a lion every day, and painted other big cats in late works like Tiger in the Snow. This artist predicted that his art would finally achieve a divine state at age 110 in the colophon to a series of books in which he called himself an "old man mad about painting." That series includes over 100 depictions of a site that this artist also depicted in prints like Fine Wind, Clear Morning and Thunderstorm Beneath the Summit. For 10 points, name this artist of Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji.

tossups.question: An artist from this country showed a general in a red dress standing on his boat and staring towards a gray mountain in one entry in a 100-painting series whose entries all depict the moon. A witch unfurls a scroll at the left side of a triptych from this country that depicts two warriors preparing to battle a gigantic skeleton. In another triptych, that artist from this country showed cats in various states of being awake and sleeping. The teal-colored bottom half of a work from this country is obscured by a blossoming (*) plum tree in the foreground. A work from this country which uses fine black lines to represent rain over a bridge was copied by Vincent van Gogh. A sky colored using Prussian Blue surrounds the central object of a work from this country, Fine Wind, Clear Morning. For 10 points, what country was home to the artist of the 53 Stations of the Tokaido?
tossups.question_sanitized: An artist from this country showed a general in a red dress standing on his boat and staring towards a gray mountain in one entry in a 100-painting series whose entries all depict the moon. A witch unfurls a scroll at the left side of a triptych from this country that depicts two warriors preparing to battle a gigantic skeleton. In another triptych, that artist from this country showed cats in various states of being awake and sleeping. The teal-colored bottom half of a work from this country is obscured by a blossoming (*) plum tree in the foreground. A work from this country which uses fine black lines to represent rain over a bridge was copied by Vincent van Gogh. A sky colored using Prussian Blue surrounds the central object of a work from this country, Fine Wind, Clear Morning. For 10 points, what country was home to the artist of the 53 Stations of the Tokaido?
tossups.answer: Japan [or Nihon; or Nippon] (The clues are all about Utagawa school artists except for Fine Wind, Clear Morning, which was painted by Hokusai.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Japan [or Nihon; or Nippon] (The clues are all about Utagawa school artists except for Fine Wind, Clear Morning, which was painted by Hokusai.)

tossups.question: This artist details how to break figures into elementary shapes, colors, and strokes in each of the three parts of his manual Quick Lessons in Simplified Drawings. Towards the end of his life, this artist painted an animal resembling a shishi featuring oddly connecting stripes “in the Snow.” This artist painted scenes of men at a stream “Fishing by Torchlight” and another depicting a whaling party in his Oceans of Wisdom series. Rippling clouds against a prussian blue sky are depicted in this artist’s (*) Fine Wind, Clear Morning. This artist used five yellow lines to depict a natural event in Thunderstorm Beneath the Summit. Three boats are shown traveling through a stormy sea in the best known work by this artist. For 10 points, name this ukiyo-e artist whose Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji includes The Great Wave off Kanagawa.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist details how to break figures into elementary shapes, colors, and strokes in each of the three parts of his manual Quick Lessons in Simplified Drawings. Towards the end of his life, this artist painted an animal resembling a shishi featuring oddly connecting stripes "in the Snow." This artist painted scenes of men at a stream "Fishing by Torchlight" and another depicting a whaling party in his Oceans of Wisdom series. Rippling clouds against a prussian blue sky are depicted in this artist's (*) Fine Wind, Clear Morning. This artist used five yellow lines to depict a natural event in Thunderstorm Beneath the Summit. Three boats are shown traveling through a stormy sea in the best known work by this artist. For 10 points, name this ukiyo-e artist whose Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji includes The Great Wave off Kanagawa.

packet.name: K – Cambridge C, Tennessee A, UCF A, UW A, UW B

tossups.question: A technique manual of “Quick Lessons” by this artist emphasizes his method that combined contour and gesture drawing. A work by this artist depicting peasants working at a water wheel shows a young child with a turtle on a leash. A known admirer of this artist, Vincent van Gogh, included many of his common subjects in the background of his Portrait of Père Tanguy (“pair tawn-GHEE”). This artist often used imported Prussian blue in a series that includes his painting Fine Wind, Clear Morning. An erotic work by this artist titled Dream of the Fisherman’s Wife depicts a diver having sex with two octopi. For 10 points, name this Japanese ukiyo-e (“oo-kee-yo-EH”) painter whose Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji includes The Great Wave off Kanagawa.
tossups.question_sanitized: A technique manual of "Quick Lessons" by this artist emphasizes his method that combined contour and gesture drawing. A work by this artist depicting peasants working at a water wheel shows a young child with a turtle on a leash. This artist often used imported Prussian blue in a series that includes his painting Fine Wind, Clear Morning. An erotic work by this artist titled Dream of the Fisherman's Wife depicts a diver having sex with two octopi. For 10 points, name this Japanese ukiyo-e ("oo-kee-yo-EH") painter whose Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji includes The Great Wave off Kanagawa.

tossups.question: This artist contrasted a floating leaf representing a peaceful mind and a snake wrapped around a tablet representing obsession beyond death in the last completed work of one series. In one work by this artist, a stack of blue plates form the neck of a spirit exiting a well; in another, this artist depicts a mutilated spirit as a paper lantern with a gaping mouth. Near the end of their life, this artist painted a tiger every day as a “daily exorcism” for good luck, such as in their hanging (+) scroll Tiger in the Snow. Memorial Anniversary and The Plate Mansion are part of this artist’s One Hundred Ghost Stories. Western painting may have influenced this artist’s use of black as a color in their series Oceans of Wisdom. This artist’s erotic series Young Pines includes an artwork with very gross dialogue from a woman being pleasured by two (*) octopi. For 10 points, name this ukiyo-e artist of The Dream of the Fisherman’s Wife.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist contrasted a floating leaf representing a peaceful mind and a snake wrapped around a tablet representing obsession beyond death in the last completed work of one series. In one work by this artist, a stack of blue plates form the neck of a spirit exiting a well; in another, this artist depicts a mutilated spirit as a paper lantern with a gaping mouth. Near the end of their life, this artist painted a tiger every day as a "daily exorcism" for good luck, such as in their hanging (+) scroll Tiger in the Snow. Memorial Anniversary and The Plate Mansion are part of this artist's One Hundred Ghost Stories. Western painting may have influenced this artist's use of black as a color in their series Oceans of Wisdom. This artist's erotic series Young Pines includes an artwork with very gross dialogue from a woman being pleasured by two (*) octopi. For 10 points, name this ukiyo-e artist of The Dream of the Fisherman's Wife.

tossups.question: One painting by this artist inspired a photograph in which leaves from two dead trees and paper thrown into the air are caught by the title Sudden Gust of Wind. In that original painting by this artist, two men clutch their hats while a third man watches his trilby blow away. In another work by this artist, three men camp alongside the banks of a waterfall emerging from a circular cliffside opening. According to legend, this artist chased a (*) chicken with red paint on its feet across a canvas to create one landscape. An elderly cooper constructs an unusually large barrel in the foreground of another painting by this artist. Small boats are threatened by the title force of nature with Mount Fuji in the background in this artist’s best-known work. For 10 points, name this Japanese ukiyo-e (“YOO-kee-OH-ey”) printmaker of Great Wave of Kanagawa.
tossups.answer: Katsushika Hokusai (the unnamed Hokusai works in power are Travellers Caught in a Sudden Breeze at Ejiri and The Waterfall of Amida behind the Kiso Road)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Katsushika Hokusai (the unnamed Hokusai works in power are Travellers Caught in a Sudden Breeze at Ejiri and The Waterfall of Amida behind the Kiso Road)

tossups.question: This artist uncharacteristically signed his name sideways and drew clouds with a solid black outline in a series of privately commissioned prints whose cover depicts a microscope analyzing a city. The Boston Museum of Fine Arts holds a painting by this artist in which a woman with her back to the viewer looks down at a mirror on a short black dresser. Possibly because his father was a mirror-maker, this artist often experimented with optics, including in a print in which a (*) mountain has no snow but its reflection does. A young boy leads a turtle on a leash in a print by this artist that depicts peasants working at a waterwheel. That print is part of a series that also depicts the title object in the distance seen through an unfinished barrel. This artist took advantage of his country’s recent import of the pigment Prussian blue for a print of a tiny mountain beneath the crest of an enormous wave. For 10 points, name this Japanese printmaker of The Great Wave off Kanagawa .
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist uncharacteristically signed his name sideways and drew clouds with a solid black outline in a series of privately commissioned prints whose cover depicts a microscope analyzing a city. That print is part of a series that also depicts the title object in the distance seen through an unfinished barrel. This artist took advantage of his country's recent import of the pigment Prussian blue for a print of a tiny mountain beneath the crest of an enormous wave. For 10 points, name this Japanese printmaker of The Great Wave off Kanagawa .

tossups.question: This artist supposedly lived in over hundred houses, since he would never clean them, instead moving to a new home until after it became too dirty. At the conclusion of one of the books, this artist predicts that when he lives to the age of 140, every stroke he paints will actually come to life. This artist legendarily painted red maple leaves on a canvas by dipping a chicken in paint and chasing it across his canvas. In one of his paintings, a peasant assembles a large (*) barrel while working in the title province. In another of his works, titled Fine Wind, Clear Morning, the title landform is shown with red hues. This artist, who specialized in a genre of woodblock painting whose name means “pictures of the floating world,” showed a large rogue wave in The Great Wave off Kanagawa. For 10 points, name this ukiyo-e artist who created “Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji.”
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist supposedly lived in over hundred houses, since he would never clean them, instead moving to a new home until after it became too dirty. In another of his works, titled Fine Wind, Clear Morning, the title landform is shown with red hues. For 10 points, name this ukiyo-e artist who created "Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji."

tossups.question: One of this artist's works depicts a gust of wind that blows away a stream of papers and hats, while another shows a craftsman sitting in a wheel-like object in the process of constructing a barrel. He once won a commission by dipping a chicken's feet in red paint and chasing it across a canvas. In one work, he painted a sunrise hitting a mountain in such a way that it turns red. More notoriously, this artist painted two octopi enveloping a diver in The Dream of the Fisherman's Wife. He incorporated various random sketches into a book called his namesake "manga." For 10 points, name this Japanese print maker who included The Great Wave Off Kanagawa in his 36 Views of Mount Fuji.
tossups.question_sanitized: One of this artist's works depicts a gust of wind that blows away a stream of papers and hats, while another shows a craftsman sitting in a wheel-like object in the process of constructing a barrel. He once won a commission by dipping a chicken's feet in red paint and chasing it across a canvas. In one work, he painted a sunrise hitting a mountain in such a way that it turns red. More notoriously, this artist painted two octopi enveloping a diver in The Dream of the Fisherman's Wife. He incorporated various random sketches into a book called his namesake "manga." For 10 points, name this Japanese print maker who included The Great Wave Off Kanagawa in his 36 Views of Mount Fuji.

bonuses.leadin: The ukiyo-e artist Sharaku primarily painted people in this profession, including a widely-memed portrait of Ōtani Oniji III. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The ukiyo-e artist Sharaku primarily painted people in this profession, including a widely-memed portrait of Otani Oniji III. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this profession of most people depicted in the yakusha-e genre, which typically showed them in distorted poses with highly caricatured faces.
This artist started out mostly depicting kabuki actors but later shifted towards depicting everyday people and landscapes, such as in his Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji.

Utagawa Kunisada painted actors and everyday people as characters from this work in his “modern,” or kinsei, series titled for it. Utagawa Kuniyoshi gained fame through his paintings of this work’s “heroes.”
parts_sanitized: Name this profession of most people depicted in the yakusha-e genre, which typically showed them in distorted poses with highly caricatured faces.
This artist started out mostly depicting kabuki actors but later shifted towards depicting everyday people and landscapes, such as in his Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji.

Utagawa Kunisada painted actors and everyday people as characters from this work in his "modern," or kinsei, series titled for it. Utagawa Kuniyoshi gained fame through his paintings of this work's "heroes."
answers: Suikoden [or Water Margin; or Outlaws of the Marsh; or Shuǐhǔ zhuàn; accept Modern Suikoden or Kinsei Suikoden or 108 Heroes of Suikoden]
answers_sanitized: actors [accept kabuki performers or noh performers; prompt on performers]
Suikoden [or Water Margin; or Outlaws of the Marsh; or Shuihu zhuan; accept Modern Suikoden or Kinsei Suikoden or 108 Heroes of Suikoden]

bonuses.leadin: According to the preface to its third volume, this series contains “images of everything [the artist] had seen or that his mind had conceived”. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: According to the preface to its third volume, this series contains "images of everything [the artist] had seen or that his mind had conceived". For 10 points each:
parts: Name this series of woodblock prints, published in fifteen volumes between 1814 and 1878. The title of this series refers to a style of drawing that is contrasted with utsushi and zu.
This artist, who began work on the Manga during a stay in Nagoya with the painter Bokusen, later elaborated on a recurring theme of its landscape prints in his Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji.

Also frequently depicted in Hokusai’s landscapes are these objects, including a “hanging” one between Hi and Etsu. Another of Hokusai’s prints depicts “One Hundred [of these objects] in a Single View.”

bonuses.leadin: This artist occasionally resorted to using coffee grounds for his drawings, one of which shows a mischievously grinning literary character with wild hair “at 11 years.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist of over four thousand drawings, some of which anticipated Surrealist automatic writing. A drawing by this artist depicts an ink-stained octopus spelling out his initials with its tentacles.
The most infamous depiction of octopi in art history is probably the erotic shunga print Dream of the Fisherman’s Wife, by this Japanese artist of The Great Wave off Kanagawa.
The curling tentacles of an octopus decorate the whole surface of a round vase by this culture, which frequently depicted a Snake Goddess in pottery figurines.

bonuses.leadin: In Benjamin Labatut’s When We Cease to Understand the World, a discussion of the staining caused by using Zyklon A as a delouser on trains transitions into a passage about this substance. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this substance. That chapter also describes how Johann Dippel’s failed attempts at soul transplantation ironically led Jacob Diesbach to create this substance when trying to make cochineal (“kotch-ih-NEEL”).
Labatut also notes this Japanese artist’s use of Prussian blue in his print The Great Wave off Kanagawa.

Kassia St. Clair’s The Secret Lives of Color describes how this scientist’s son John used Prussian blue to make the first blueprints. This astronomer’s sister Caroline discovered eight comets using his large reflecting telescopes.
answers: William Herschel [or Frederick William Herschel; or Friedrich Wilhelm Herschel]
answers_sanitized: William Herschel [or Frederick William Herschel; or Friedrich Wilhelm Herschel]

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about the artistic inspirations for Jeff Wall’s photographs, for 10 points each.
parts: In a Wall photograph (emphasize) inspired by this painting, a woman’s destroyed room contains a dresser with the drawers pulled out and an overturned mattress. Sculptural depictions of elephant heads protrude from the corners of the central object in this 19th-century painting.

A man and woman stand in front of a mirror in Wall’s Picture for Women, which was inspired by this painting. A bowl of oranges sits in the foreground of this painting, which depicts a prostitute wearing a pendant on a black necklace reflected by a mirror.
Wall’s photograph A Sudden Gust of Wind was inspired by this Japanese artist, who painted Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji.
answers: The Death of Sardanapalus [or La Mort de Sardanapale] (by Eugéne Delacroix)
A Bar at the Folies-Bergére [or Un bar aux Folies Bergère] (by Édouard Manet)

packet.name: M_ Princeton, Edinburgh, Duke B, Maryland A, Kenyon, Cambridge B

answers_sanitized: The Death of Sardanapalus [or La Mort de Sardanapale] (by Eugene Delacroix)
A Bar at the Folies-Bergere [or Un bar aux Folies Bergere] (by Edouard Manet)

bonuses.leadin: This artist sketched a horned man with eyestalks wrapping around his face against a backdrop of chains while still at Polaris High School. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist of that portfolio, which appeared on a 2019 Antiques Roadshow episode. This artist tore down wood-gridded domes inspired by Tatooine, meant for the homeless, after failing to obtain a proper building permit.

Kanye collaborated with Takashi Murakami for the cover art of Kids See Ghosts, in which wispy white figures hover at the center of a landscape inspired by this Japanese artist of TheGreat Wave off Kanagawa.

One version of this Kanye album’s cover features a ballerina in a black tutu holding a wine glass, while an uncensored version depicts Kanye straddled by an armless winged female nude, referred to as a phoenix.
answers: Kanye West [or Kanye Omari West; prompt on Ye or Yeezy or Yeezus]

packet.name: I - Oxford B, UNC A, WUSTL D, Kentucky A, Stanford A, Minnesota B

bonuses.leadin: An artist known for painting these objects promised to have sex with Richard Nixon if he would stop the Vietnam War. For 10 points each:
parts: Yayoi Kusama often paints enormous numbers of these things in many forms, inspired by her hallucinations. She describes them as “a way to infinity.”

This other contemporary Japanese artist started the “superflat” movement, blurring “high” and “low” culture. He paints scary-looking teddy bears which have been seen on Louis Vuitton bags and Kanye West albums.
This somewhat less contemporary Japanese artist created woodblock prints showing Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, including one in which it is overshadowed by a large wave.

bonuses.leadin: This artist's Night Rain on Karasaki and Evening Glow at Seta are part of his series on Lake Biwa titled Eight Views of Omi. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Japanese artist, who traveled along a road from Edo to Kyoto to create his series Fifty-Three Stations of the Tokaido.
Hiroshige was a master of ukiyo-e, as was this other artist whose Thirty-Six Views of Mount Fuji include The Great Wave off Kanagawa.
Another popular form of Japanese art is found in byobu, which are this type of object. One made in Hikone, an early example of ukiyo-e, is considered a National Treasure of Japan.

bonuses.leadin: This artist supposedly made a painting featuring maple leaves by dipping a chicken’s feet in paint and chasing it across the canvas. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who is better known for his series Thirty-Six Views of Mt. Fuji.

Hokusai’s Eagle in a Snowstorm is a painting made on one of these objects. In the medieval art form of Emakimono these things are illustrated with narratives; one such example is the Genji Monogatari Emaki.

This style of painting is contrasted with the Chinese style tara-e, meaning “Tang painting.” It now refers to a style of Japanese paintings featuring exaggerated facial expressions and fukinuki yatai, or houses with their roofs removed so that their interiors can be seen.
